name: English Muffin Batter Bread
recipeIngredient: 5 ½ cups King Arthur Unbleached All-Purpose Flour, to 6 C
recipeInstructions:
This is a yeasty, coarse-textured bread. It makes great toast and a perfect partner to fresh summer jam or preserves. This is a purely mix-it-slap-in-the-pan-bake-and-eat-it loaf. It's a particularly nice loaf in the summer, when you might not feel like spending a lot of time in the kitchen.
indicates items that can be purchased from King Arthur Flour Co.
1. Combine 3 cups of the flour, yeast, sugar, salt, and baking soda.
2. Heat the milk and water until very warm, about 120°F.
3. Pour the liquids into the dry mixture, and beat well.
4. Mix in enough of the remaining flour to make a stiff dough.
5. Grease two 8½ x 4½-inch bread pans and sprinkle them with cornmeal.
6. Spoon the dough into the pans. Sprinkle additional cornmeal on top of the loaves.
7. Cover and let rise in a warm place for 45 minutes.
8. Bake at 400°F for 25 minutes. Remove loaves from pans and cool on a wire rack.
Makes 2 loaves.
